name: Abney, Fulton Swanzy
motto: "Only in the world to fill up a place."
description: Frequents the library often, reading Latin translations. His greatest achievement was "pink eye" during his Senior year.

name: Austin, Oliver Vastine
motto: "Let's have wine, woman, mirth and laughter. Sermons and soda water the day after."
description: Commonly known as "Spout," and one time elected as the busiest knocker. Studying is his hobby.

name: Brown, Julia Clementine
motto: "'Tis not what I do that comforts me, but what I would do."
description: Wants her own way. Doesn't bother about consequences. Wants everything that U. of M. can give her.

name: Coulter, Bayard Lamar
motto: "With smiles and wine and kisses, She'll help him have his fling, And to his purse she won't do A solitary thing."
description: Doesn't like to have his picture made. Loved all the co-eds., but has never made a hit.

name: Guyton, Billie S.
motto: "None but the brave deserve the fair."
major: M.D., B.S., M.A., Mississippi College 1908, M.A. U. of M. 1911

name: Huston, Mary Emma
motto: "Satisfied with a dozen oysters."
description: Spent her youth eating oysters, onions and drinking buttermilk. She is little but loud.

name: Johnston, Samuel McCoy
motto: "Imagination of some great exploit Drives him beyond the bounds of patience."
description: Orator of the Demosthenian type, and in his flights makes frequent visits to the planet Mars. Member of the "Old Guard."

name: Moore, Newton Augustus
motto: "Whosoever findeth a wife, findeth a good thing."
description: An orator so eloquent that he persuaded a woman to marry him. Will become a missionary.

name: Pierce, Millard Franklin
motto: "I'll sacrifice the lamb that I do love, To spite a raven's heart within a dove."
description: Is studying law and hopes to distinguish himself as a Justice of the Peace. Loves Freshman history and is very fond of coconuts.

name: Smith, John T.
motto: "In peace he is the lamb in the meadow, but in war the lion of the forest."
description: Likes a funny joke. Has already entered politics. Loves to hear from Columbus. "Has taken all knowledge to be his sphere."

name: Smallwood, Lillie Belle
motto: "Not too good for human nature's daily food."
description: Is given to fighting. She understands the art of bugging professors.

name: Smith, Robbie
motto: "There are none like her, none."
description: Tall, graceful, sweet. A heart smasher. The roses upon her cheeks have never faded away.

name: Thompson, William Elmer
motto: "As mild a mannered man as ever scuttled a ship or cut a throat."
description: "I had rather be Chancery Clerk of Attala County than President of the United States." He is trying to take all the degrees that the University can confer.

name: Tipton, Samuel Powell
motto: "Not in the role of common men."
description: Spends most of his time waiting for the mail. "Say, kid, what does it cost to go to Laurel?"

name: Rucker, James Dorman
motto: "The noblest Roman of them all."
description: Most of the co-eds are crazy about him. He loves Math., but adores Chemistry.

name: Simms, Alma
motto: "Knowledge is the true path to the domain of truth."
description: She is one of the very few M.A. students that loves studying because it feeds the brain.

name: Glass, David Henry
motto: "Look not upon wine when it is red, for it biteth like an adder and stingeth like a serpent."
description: An all around man. The hairs of his head are numbered. He is a statesman in an embryonic stage.

name: Storer, Annie Rue
motto: "O'er books consumes the midnight oil."
description: If silence be riches, she is the daughter of Croesus. Believes that Kosciusko is Paradise lost. Wants an M.D. degree, of course.

name: Kendell, Julia Lestine
motto: "A rosy cheek and a broad smile."
description: Fond of history; isn't afraid of Dr. Riley. Never had a serious thought.

name: Kendell, Alpha
motto: "The faint perfume alone is in the virgin air."
description: Talks like a baby. Knows how to be silent-in history. Will be a blessing to a home.

name: Leveret, Josie
motto: "I am of the church."
description: Bugs Dr. Riley after history class every day, when she ought to do so before. Converting heathen co-eds is her pastime.

name: Rayburn, Stephen Banks
motto: "May the links that are lost but endear what are left."
description: Freshman Math. is his favorite study. As an orator he is without a peer (?).

name: Crawley, David E.
motto: "He thinks too much; such men are dangerous."
description: A strong prohibitionist (?) Dave is true blue, having insuperable nerve, as he was one of the few to protest against the faculty encroaching upon the rights of the students. He will be the next Senator from Attala County.

name: Mize, Sidney Carr
motto: "I dare do all that may become a man, who dares to do more is none."
description: Great possibilities are wrapped within this man. Will graduate with distinction at the head of his class.

name: Nichols, Raymond Lafayette
motto: "O Jupiter, how weary are my spirits."
description: Nick loves equity more than any other study in Senior Law. He was the happiest man in school after the second term exams. Believes that Mississippi College is the Harvard of the South.

name: Simmons, Joe A.
motto: "A jewel in a ten-times barr'd-up chest Is a bold spirit in a loyal breast."
clubs: Leader of Democratic Party U. of M. Congress
description: Joe is a man who absorbs enough law to stay around the head of his class. He objects to studying and goes to classes only to keep from getting demerits.

name: Smith, John Timothy
motto: "So wise, so grave, of so perplexed a tongue and loud withal that would not wag nor scarce lie still without a fee."

name: Denman, Richard
motto: "We thank the gods our Rome has such a soldier."
description: "Dick" is the orator of the University, and will no doubt clear his county of all criminals when he is elected County Attorney next fall. We are looking for him and "Cattle-gap" Schauber to mop up with L. S. U. Debating Team this year.

name: McCall, John W.
motto: "A man without fear and without reproach."
description: "Scotchy" is a true athlete, representing the moral and intellectual side as well as the athletic. He is one of the most popular students in the University.

name: Carter, Edward Bunyan
motto: "Truth crushed to earth will rise again."
description: A strong adherent to the "White Chief." He is a politician from the core. Delights in his wavy locks.

name: Cleveland, T. Grover
motto:
"I will through and through
Cleanse the foul body of the infected world,
If they will patiently receive my medicine."

name: Edmondson, Henry G.
motto:
"Laugh, and the world laughs with you;
Weep and you weep alone."

name: Fernandez, Julius Raymond
motto:
"Our fancies are more giddy and unfirm,
More longing, wavering, sooner lost and won,
Than women's are."

name: Hunt, W. R.
motto: "Needles and pins, needles and pins, When a man marries his trouble begins."

name: Bramlett, William B.
motto: "I shape my means to fit my ends."

name: Robinson, Charley Watson
motto: "Live man, well man, dead man, stiff, Dig 'em up, cut 'em up, what's the diff? Humorous, tumorous, blood and gore, I will be an M.D. in two years more."

name: Furr, James Edward
motto: "With great reluctance do I deviate from the phraseology of my books."

name: Duggins, Percy E.
motto: "Gentle and modest as a flower."

name: Dobbins, V. L.
motto: "Methinks I am more honest than wise."

name: Randolph, V. P.
motto: "No man is at all times wise."

name: James, W. A. DeWitt
motto: "Women are as roses, whose fair flower, Being once displayed, doth fall that very hour."

name: Linton, A. M.
clubs: Officers of the Senior Pharmacy Class

name: Gresham, Ollie Roscoe
motto: "Another good farmer spoiled."

name: Knox, John Creagis
motto: "His words are bonds, his oaths are oracles, His love sincere, his thoughts immaculate."

name: Linton, Anderson Maltruverse
motto: "'Tis well said, friend, truly this is a lemon."

name: Miller, Cecil Edward
motto: "He never broke any man's head but his own, and that was against a post."

name: Russell, Lloyd Roselle
motto: "My drugs will effect great cures."
